hypothesis: Incorporating stereochemical diversity constraints into NaviDiv's adaptive penalty framework will reveal that reinforcement learning optimization for chiral molecular targets exhibits a stereochemical collapse phenomenon distinct from structural collapse—where the model converges on a single enantiomeric or diastereomeric preference well before overall fingerprint-based diversity metrics detect any significant loss—and that adding explicit stereocentre-aware penalties to the combined constraint regime will recover enantiodiverse candidate libraries while maintaining property optimization performance comparable to the fragment-only constraint regime.
